```{r, echo=FALSE, results="hide"}
set.seed(sample.int(.Machine$integer.max, 1))

## Parâmetros aleatórios
mu         <- sample(2:7, 1)
n          <- sample(c(25, 36, 50, 100), 1)
sigma2_true <- sample(c(1, 2, 4, 9), 1)
sum_sq     <- round(n * sigma2_true + rnorm(1, 0, 2), 2)
## Garantir sum_sq > 0
sum_sq <- max(sum_sq, 0.5)

## Resposta correta
correct <- round(sum_sq / n, 4)

## Alternativas incorretas plausíveis
w1 <- round(sum_sq / (n - 1), 4)
w2 <- round(sum_sq / (n + 1), 4)
w3 <- round(sqrt(sum_sq / n), 4)
w4 <- round(sum_sq / n + 1, 4)

## Garantir unicidade
if (w1 == correct) w1 <- round(sum_sq / (n - 2), 4)
if (w2 == correct) w2 <- round(sum_sq / (n + 2), 4)
if (w2 == w1)      w2 <- round(sum_sq / (n + 3), 4)
if (w3 == correct || w3 == w1 || w3 == w2) w3 <- round(sqrt(sum_sq / n) + 0.1, 4)
if (w4 == correct || w4 == w1 || w4 == w2 || w4 == w3) w4 <- round(sum_sq / n - 1, 4)

## Embaralhamento SEMPRE
all_vals <- c(correct, w1, w2, w3, w4)
# Garante cinco alternativas numericamente distintas após a parametrização.
integer_choices <- all(abs(all_vals - round(all_vals)) < sqrt(.Machine$double.eps))
choice_step <- if (integer_choices) 1 else max(1e-4, max(abs(all_vals), na.rm = TRUE) * 0.01)
while (anyDuplicated(all_vals)) {
  duplicate_position <- which(duplicated(all_vals))[1]
  all_vals[duplicate_position] <- max(all_vals, na.rm = TRUE) + choice_step
}
choices <- sample(all_vals)
sol_pos <- which(choices == correct)
sol_str <- paste(as.integer(seq_along(choices) == sol_pos), collapse = "")
```

Question
========

Seja $X_i \overset{\text{iid}}{\sim} N(`r mu`, \sigma^2)$ com $\mu = `r mu`$ conhecido. O EMV de $\sigma^2$ é $\hat{\sigma}^2_{MV} = \frac{1}{n}\sum_{i=1}^n (X_i - `r mu`)^2$. Dado $n = `r n`$ e $\sum_{i=1}^n (X_i - `r mu`)^2 = `r sum_sq`$, calcule $\hat{\sigma}^2_{MV}$.

Answerlist
----------

* `r choices[1]`
* `r choices[2]`
* `r choices[3]`
* `r choices[4]`
* `r choices[5]`

Solution
========

A log-verossimilhança para $\sigma^2$ com $\mu$ conhecido é $\ell(\sigma^2) = -\frac{n}{2}\log\sigma^2 - \frac{1}{2\sigma^2}\sum(X_i - `r mu`)^2$. Derivando e igualando a zero:
$$\frac{d\ell}{d\sigma^2} = -\frac{n}{2\sigma^2} + \frac{\sum(X_i - `r mu`)^2}{2(\sigma^2)^2} = 0 \implies \hat{\sigma}^2_{MV} = \frac{\sum(X_i - `r mu`)^2}{n} = \frac{`r sum_sq`}{`r n`} = `r correct`.$$
Note que o divisor é $n$, não $n-1$ (como na variância amostral não viesada $S^2$).

Meta-information
================
exname: Cap3S2Q2_emv_normal_sigma2
extype: schoice
exsolution: `r sol_str`
exshuffle: FALSE
